Implements an interface for the MCP23009 I2C GPIO expander, including setup, configuration, and control functions.

# Purpose
The code defines a class `Mcp23009` that provides an interface for interacting with the MCP23009 I/O expander over an I2C bus. The class includes methods to configure and control the I/O pins of the MCP23009 device. It allows setting up the I2C bus connection, configuring GPIO directions, enabling or disabling USB charging, and setting accessory pull-ups. The class also includes methods to read and write to the device's registers, reset the device to its default state, and read the current state of all registers.

The `Mcp23009` class is designed to be used in environments where control over multiple I/O pins is needed, such as in embedded systems or hardware interfacing applications. The class provides methods like [`setButtons`](<#Mcp23009setButtons>), [`configureGPIODirection`](<#Mcp23009configureGPIODirection>), [`setUsbChargeEn`](<#Mcp23009setUsbChargeEn>), and [`setAccessoryPullup`](<#Mcp23009setAccessoryPullup>) to manipulate the state of the I/O pins. The class uses the `struct` module for packing and unpacking data to and from the I2C bus, and it handles the acknowledgment process to ensure successful communication with the device. The code is intended to be part of a larger system where it can be imported and used to manage the MCP23009 I/O expander.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `struct.*`
- `array.array`
- `time`


# Global Variables

---
### REG\_IODIR
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the I/O direction register in the MCP23009 I2C I/O expander. This register is used to configure the direction of the GPIO pins as either input or output.
- **Use**: Used to read from or write to the I/O direction register to configure GPIO pin directions.


---
### REG\_IPOL
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the input polarity register in the MCP23009 I/O expander. This register is used to configure the polarity of the input pins.
- **Use**: Used to read from or write to the input polarity register of the MCP23009 device.


---
### REG\_GPINTEN
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the GPIO interrupt-on-change enable register in the MCP23009 I/O expander. This register is used to enable or disable interrupt-on-change for each pin.
- **Use**: Used to specify the register address when reading from or writing to the GPIO interrupt-on-change enable register in the MCP23009.


---
### REG\_DEFVAL
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the default value register address for the MCP23009 I/O expander. It is used to set the default comparison value for interrupt-on-change pins.
- **Use**: Used in the `reset` method to initialize the default value register to zero.


---
### REG\_INTCON
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: A hexadecimal integer constant that represents the address of the interrupt control register in the MCP23009 I/O expander.
- **Use**: Used to specify the register address for interrupt control operations in I2C communication with the MCP23009.


---
### REG\_IOCON
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: A hexadecimal integer constant that represents the address of the IOCON register in the MCP23009 I/O expander.
- **Use**: Used to specify the IOCON register address when reading from or writing to the I/O expander.


---
### REG\_GPPU
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the GPIO pull-up resistor configuration in the MCP23009 I/O expander. The value `0x06` is used to access this specific register.
- **Use**: Used to configure the pull-up resistors for the GPIO pins in the MCP23009 device.


---
### REG\_INTF
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the interrupt flag register in the MCP23009 I/O expander. This register is used to determine which pins have triggered an interrupt.
- **Use**: Used in I2C communication to read or write the interrupt flag register of the MCP23009.


---
### REG\_INTCAP
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the interrupt capture register in the MCP23009 I/O expander. This register captures the GPIO port value at the time of an interrupt.
- **Use**: Used to read the captured GPIO port value when an interrupt occurs.


---
### REG\_GPIO
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents the register address for the GPIO (General Purpose Input/Output) in the MCP23009 I2C I/O expander. The value `0x09` is used to access the GPIO register for reading or writing the state of the I/O pins.
- **Use**: Used to read from or write to the GPIO register in the MCP23009 I2C I/O expander.


---
### REG\_OLAT
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: A hexadecimal constant that represents the register address for the output latch (OLAT) in the MCP23009 I/O expander. The value `0x0a` is used to access the OLAT register, which controls the output levels of the GPIO pins.
- **Use**: Used to read from or write to the output latch register in the MCP23009 I/O expander.


---
### ACK
- **Type**: ``int``
- **Description**: Represents an integer value used as a constant to indicate a successful acknowledgment in I2C communication. The value is set to 0, which is typically used to signify a successful operation or acknowledgment in many communication protocols.
- **Use**: Used to compare against the acknowledgment response from the I2C bus to determine if the communication was successful.

#### Mcp23009\.\_\_init\_\_<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.__init__}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L36>)

Initializes an instance of the `Mcp23009` class with a specified I2C address.
- **Inputs**:
    - `address`: The I2C address for the device, which is used to set the write address by masking the least significant bit.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the `i2cBus` attribute to `None`, indicating that the I2C bus is not yet initialized.
    - Calculates the write address by performing a bitwise AND operation on the provided `address` with `0xFE`, effectively clearing the least significant bit, and assigns it to the `i2cAddress` attribute.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a constructor method.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.setup<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.setup}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L41>)

Configures the I2C bus and sets up the initial state of the I/O expander.
- **Inputs**:
    - `i2cBus`: An I2C bus object used for communication with the I/O expander.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the provided `i2cBus` to the instance variable `self.i2cBus`.
    - Reads the current I/O direction register (`REG_IODIR`) and masks it to configure specific pins as outputs.
    - Writes the modified I/O direction back to the `REG_IODIR` register.
    - Writes a value to the `REG_GPPU` register to enable the pull-up resistor on a specific pin.
- **Output**: No explicit return value; modifies the state of the I/O expander through I2C communication.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.\_i2cWrite8BitReg<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L52>)

Writes an 8-bit value to a specified I2C register address.
- **Inputs**:
    - `regAddress`: The address of the register to write to.
    - `regValue`: The 8-bit value to write to the register.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Starts the I2C communication by calling `self.i2cBus.Start()`.
    - Packs the I2C address, register address, and register value into a byte string using `pack('>BBB', self.i2cAddress, regAddress, regValue)`.
    - Writes the packed byte string to the I2C bus using `self.i2cBus.Write(writeString)`.
    - Checks for acknowledgment from the I2C device using `self.i2cBus.GetAck()`.
    - Prints a message if no acknowledgment is received.
    - Stops the I2C communication by calling `self.i2cBus.Stop()`.
- **Output**: None
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)


---
#### Mcp23009\.\_i2cRead8BitReg<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L64>)

Reads an 8-bit register from an I2C device using the I2C bus.
- **Inputs**:
    - `regAddress`: The address of the register to read from the I2C device.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initiates an I2C start condition using `self.i2cBus.Start()`.
    - Prepares a write string with the I2C device address and the register address using `pack('>BB', self.i2cAddress, regAddress)` and writes it to the bus.
    - Checks for acknowledgment from the I2C device using `self.i2cBus.GetAck()` and prints a message if no acknowledgment is received.
    - Initiates another I2C start condition to switch to read mode by writing the device address with a read bit set using `pack('B', (self.i2cAddress + 0x01))`.
    - Checks for acknowledgment again and prints a message if no acknowledgment is received.
    - Sends a NACK to the I2C bus using `self.i2cBus.SendNacks()` to prepare for reading data.
    - Reads one byte of data from the I2C bus using `self.i2cBus.Read(1)`.
    - Sends an ACK to the I2C bus using `self.i2cBus.SendAcks()` after reading the data.
    - Stops the I2C communication using `self.i2cBus.Stop()`.
- **Output**: Returns the data read from the specified register as a byte.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.setButtons<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.setButtons}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L85>)

Configures the state of specific buttons by modifying the GPIO register.
- **Inputs**:
    - `back`: A boolean flag to set the 'back' button state.
    - `up`: A boolean flag to set the 'up' button state.
    - `down`: A boolean flag to set the 'down' button state.
    - `select`: A boolean flag to set the 'select' button state.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Reads the current state of the GPIO register and applies a mask to isolate button bits.
    - Prints the current GPIO state before modification.
    - Checks each button flag ('back', 'up', 'down', 'select') and modifies the corresponding bit in the GPIO register to set the button state.
    - Prints the modified GPIO state after setting the buttons.
    - Writes the modified GPIO state back to the GPIO register.
- **Output**: No explicit return value; modifies the GPIO register to set button states.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)

- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.configureGPIODirection<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.configureGPIODirection}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L100>)

Configures the direction of GPIO pins on the MCP23009 device as input or output based on a given mask.
- **Inputs**:
    - `gpio_mask`: A bitmask indicating which GPIO pins to configure as input or output.
    - `as_output`: A boolean flag indicating whether to set the specified GPIO pins as output (True) or input (False). Defaults to True.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Reads the current GPIO direction register value using [`_i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>) with `REG_IODIR` as the address.
    - Checks if `as_output` is True; if so, it clears the bits in `gpiodir` specified by `gpio_mask` to set them as outputs.
    - If `as_output` is False, it sets the bits in `gpiodir` specified by `gpio_mask` to configure them as inputs.
    - Compares the modified `gpiodir` with the original value; if they differ, writes the new value back to the GPIO direction register using [`_i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>).
    - Reads back the GPIO direction register to confirm the change and prints the new register value.
- **Output**: None. The function performs I/O operations and prints the updated GPIO direction register value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.setUsbChargeEn<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.setUsbChargeEn}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L114>)

Controls the USB charging enable state by configuring GPIO direction and updating the GPIO register.
- **Inputs**:
    - `chargeEnable`: A boolean flag that indicates whether to enable (True) or disable (False) USB charging.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set `usb_en_mask` to 0x10, which represents the USB enable mask.
    - Call [`configureGPIODirection`](<#Mcp23009configureGPIODirection>) with `usb_en_mask` to set the GPIO direction for USB charging.
    - Read the current GPIO register value using [`_i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>) and mask out the USB V+ enable bit using `& 0xEF`.
    - Print the current GPIO value before modification.
    - If `chargeEnable` is True, set the USB enable bit in `curGPIO` using `|= usb_en_mask`.
    - Print the modified GPIO value after setting the USB enable bit.
    - Write the modified GPIO value back to the GPIO register using [`_i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>).
- **Output**: No explicit return value; modifies the state of the GPIO register to enable or disable USB charging.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.configureGPIODirection`](<#Mcp23009configureGPIODirection>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.setAccessoryPullup<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.setAccessoryPullup}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L130>)

Configures the accessory pull-up resistor state on the GPIO register.
- **Inputs**:
    - `pullupEnable`: A boolean that indicates whether to enable (True) or disable (False) the accessory pull-up resistor.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set the accessory enable mask to 0x20.
    - Call [`configureGPIODirection`](<#Mcp23009configureGPIODirection>) with the accessory enable mask to set the GPIO direction.
    - Read the current GPIO register value using [`_i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>).
    - Determine if the accessory pull-up is currently enabled by checking if the masked GPIO value is 0.
    - Compare the current state with the desired state (`pullupEnable`).
    - If the current state differs from the desired state, clear the current setting by applying a bitwise AND with the negated mask.
    - If `pullupEnable` is False, set the accessory enable bit by applying a bitwise OR with the mask.
    - Write the updated GPIO value back to the register using [`_i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>).
    - Read the GPIO register again to confirm the change and print the result.
- **Output**: Prints the current value of the GPIO register after attempting to set the accessory pull-up state.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.configureGPIODirection`](<#Mcp23009configureGPIODirection>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)

#### Mcp23009\.reset<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.reset}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L148>)

Resets all registers of the MCP23009 I2C I/O expander to their default states.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Writes the value `0xFF` to the `REG_IODIR` register to set all pins as inputs.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_IPOL` register to set the input polarity to non-inverted.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_GPINTEN` register to disable interrupt-on-change for all pins.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_DEFVAL` register to clear the default comparison value for interrupt-on-change.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_INTCON` register to set interrupt control to compare against previous value.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_IOCON` register to reset the I/O configuration register.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_GPPU` register to disable the pull-up resistors on all pins.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_INTF` register to clear the interrupt flag register.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_INTCAP` register to clear the interrupt capture register.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_GPIO` register to clear the GPIO register.
    - Writes the value `0x00` to the `REG_OLAT` register to clear the output latch register.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cWrite8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cWrite8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)


---
#### Mcp23009\.readRegs<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009.readRegs}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.py#L162>)

Reads and prints the values of the first 11 registers from the I2C device.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Iterates over a range of register addresses from 0 to 10.
    - For each register address, calls the [`_i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>) method to read the register value.
    - Unpacks the read byte to an integer value.
    - Prints the register address and its corresponding value in hexadecimal format.
- **Output**: No return value; outputs register addresses and values to the console.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009._i2cRead8BitReg`](<#Mcp23009_i2cRead8BitReg>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/tools/power_monitor/mcp23009.Mcp23009`](<#Mcp23009>)  (Base Class)
